changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 361-366 (Phase 4 question 5); signal_evaluator.py lines 380-420 (mandatory_pre_emission_gates mechanism); context_modifier_catalogue_draft_v1.yaml line 41 (runtime_active=false for mod_demo_age)
changes.detail: Phase 4 question 5 asks 'can FT3 low be safely emitted as a strictly gated or context-missing state?' The existing mandatory_pre_emission_gates mechanism could technically enforce TSH+FT4 biomarker_present gates — but this would NOT satisfy the clinical sign-off requirement. The sign-off requires illness/medication context which is not runtime-consumed. STOP condition 5 is the binding constraint and overrides question 5.
changes.hardening_instruction: Cursor must follow the preferred safe outcome stated in Phase 4: 'Keep FT3 low inactive. Record formal blocker: ft3_low_requires_layer_b_context.' TSH+FT4 biomarker_present gates without illness/medication context evaluation do NOT satisfy the clinical sign-off requirement and must not be implemented in this sprint. Do not partially activate FT3 low.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED AVAILABLE BUT NOT NEEDED THIS SPRINT
changes.detail: The mandatory_pre_emission_gates mechanism is real, fail-closed (missing gate input returns False at line 407), and integrated in evaluate_all. It supports biomarker_present (line 400) and lab_range_boundary comparators. Currently used by the three activated thyroid packages. The mechanism exists for future use — but all three STOP conditions prevent any new activation in this sprint.

CF-BATCH2-007 (eGFR adjudication): Open — requires BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1. CF-BATCH2-010 (androgen clinical sign-off): Open — blocks all 8 androgen packages before re-promotion. CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 (runtime context evaluation): Open — prerequisite for androgen execution and FT3 low activation. Prompt correctly requires updating all three. CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 should be updated to include FT3-low illness context as an explicit blocker within scope.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED — ALL THREE KNOWN BLOCKERS TRIGGER STOP
changes.detail: STOP condition 3 (eGFR double-counting): fires — no anti-double-counting mechanism in evaluator. STOP condition 4 (androgen context not runtime-consumed): fires — all catalogue modifiers runtime_active=false, no context modifier evaluation in SignalEvaluator. STOP condition 5 (FT3 low illness/medication context not runtime-consumed): fires — CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 open, runtime_consumed=false throughout catalogue. Expected sprint outcome: zero activations; all 11 packages formally blocked with registered blockers; execution register and audit report created; carry-forward register updated.

changes.detail: Regression targets: all 11 in-scope packages must remain inactive; the 6 already-active Batch 2 packages must remain unaffected. If zero activations (expected), tests are only required for any changed runtime/gating code — and no runtime/gating code should change. Out-of-scope list is comprehensive: clinical wording, thresholds, reference ranges, signal IDs, activation keys, frontend, SSOT, scoring, domain score assembly, report compiler, ranking. No unintended behavioural drift is possible if STOP conditions are respected. Mandatory STOP gate before any activation is appropriately placed.
